channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mamata Banerjee Confident of TMC Securing Over 200 Seats in West Bengal Assembly Elections
item.description: Ahead of the counting day for West Bengal assembly elections on May 4, 2026, Chief Minister and TMC leader Mamata Banerjee expressed strong confidence that her party will secure more than 200 seats. S...

item.title: Repolling conducted at 15 booths in South 24 Parganas after irregularities, high turnout recorded
item.description: Repolling was conducted on Saturday at 15 polling stations in West Bengal’s South 24 Parganas district following the Election Commission’s decision to void earlier voting due to reported irregularitie...

item.title: Lethbridge Fire and EMS System at Risk of Being Disbanded
item.description: Lethbridge, Alberta, has had an integrated firefighter and paramedic service for over 114 years. However, recent changes by the provincial government could threaten this model, which may cease to exis...

item.title: NHAI opens first arm of elevated corridor on Chennai–Bengaluru Highway at Melvisharam to ease congestion
item.description: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has opened one arm of a new elevated corridor on the Chennai–Bengaluru Highway at Melvisharam in Tamil Nadu, a move aimed at easing traffic congestion a...

item.title: CILT Nigeria partners with EFCC to strengthen transport sector integrity
item.description: The Chartered Institute of Logistics and Transportation (CILT), Nigeria, don reach out to the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) to forge a strategic partnership. This move, led by the CI...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari's Mother Shares Her Experience While Supporting His Kasarani MP Bid
item.description: Pastor Kanyari has officially started his campaigns for the Kasarani parliamentary seat, and this time, he brought along his mother, Prophetess Lucy Nduta, to support him. Kanyari, who recently announ...

item.title: Anthony Marsh secures Nepean seat as Liberals prepare for Victorian state election
item.description: The Liberal Party has secured a win in the Nepean byelection in Victoria, providing a significant boost to opposition leader Jess Wilson ahead of the state elections in November. With around 80% of vo...

item.title: West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026: Key Leaders and Factors Shaping the Contest
item.description: The 2026 West Bengal assembly elections are shaping up as a closely fought contest between the ruling Trinamool Congress (TMC) and the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). Exit polls show a divided mandate, ...

item.title: Ford Issues Recall on Thousands of Vehicles Over Seat Safety Concern
item.description: Ford Motor Company has issued a recall affecting thousands of its vehicles due to a safety issue related to moving seats. The problem, which could potentially compromise driver and passenger safety, i...

item.title: US Plans Gradual Withdrawal of 5,000 Troops from Germany Amid Rising Transatlantic Tensions
item.description: NATO has said it is consulting closely with the United States to understand the tafseel of Washington’s decision to withdraw around 5,000 troops from Germany, a move that comes at a sensitive time for...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Refuses to Relinquish Nuclear and Missile Capabilities
item.description: In a rare public statement, Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, declared that Iran would not abandon its nuclear and missile capabilities. The statement, read on Iranian state televisio...

item.title: Karachi experiences ongoing heatwave with temperatures expected to rise further
item.description: Karachi is currently experiencing very hot weather, with the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) reporting a maximum temperature of 38.5°C on Saturday. The PMD has warned that the heat will likel...

item.title: US Appeals Court Suspends Mail Delivery of Abortion Pill Nationwide
item.description: On Friday, one US appeals court temporarily stop women from getting mifepristone, the main abortion medicine, through mail. The court, wey get conservative judges, give the order after Louisiana, one ...

item.title: Bauchi Governor Bala Mohammed Defects from PDP to APM Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Bauchi State Governor, Bala Mohammed don officially announce say e don leave the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) go join the Allied Peoples Movement (APM). The announcement happen for Bauchi Government...

item.title: AI Deepfakes Distort Wildlife Behavior, Impacting Public Perception and Conservation Efforts
item.description: AI-generated videos are spreading across social media, creating deepfakes of wildlife, including the famous Big Bear bald eagles Jackie and Shadow. These videos often depict unrealistic or fabricated ...

item.title: Austrian police detain suspect in baby food poisoning and extortion case
item.description: Austrian authorities have arrested a 39-year-old man in connection with a disturbing case involving the alleged contamination of baby food with rat poison. According to police, the suspect was apprehe...

item.title: Sifuna Encourages Kenyan Workers Amid Economic Challenges on Labour Day
item.description: On Labour Day 2026, ODM Secretary-General Edwin Sifuna addressed Kenyan workers with a short but politically tinged message. In a post on X, the Nairobi Senator urged workers to stay strong despite on...

item.title: Tanzania connects labour reforms and job creation to Vision 2050 as workers demand full enforcement
item.description: During the 2026 Workers’ Day celebrations held in Njombe, the Tanzanian government highlighted its ongoing efforts to connect labour reforms, job creation strategies and the long-term Vision 2050 agen...

item.title: Sagar Adani Advocates Complete Electrification to Strengthen India's Resilience
item.description: In a recent statement, industrialist Sagar Adani emphasised that India's most effective strategy to mitigate global risks is to focus on comprehensive electrification across all sectors. He suggested ...

item.title: Bomas of Kenya Conference Centre Completion Delayed to September 2026
item.description: The completion of the Bomas of Kenya International Conference Centre (BICC) has now been rescheduled to September 2026, after an inspection by the Senate Committee on Roads, Transportation and Infrast...
